'''Toafofoa Sipley''' (born 5 January 1995) is a [[Niue national rugby league team|Niue international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the NRL.

==Background==
Sipley was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/696502307067427/photos/a.1073903632660624.1073742088.696502307067427/1073903942660593/?type=3&theater|title=Nesian Power - Missing Profiles... Shaun Kenny-Dowall,...|publisher=Facebook|date=2016-02-25|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> and [[Niue]]an<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/NiueRugbyLeague/photos/a.480984731948962.97392.450707424976693/1015986785115418/?type=3&theater|title=Congratulations Toafofoa Sipley on your... - Niue Rugby League|publisher=Facebook|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> descent. 

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Richmond Rovers]], before being signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/81090073/warriors-rookie-toafofoa-sipley-ready-to-make-his-mark-after-long-layoff |title=Warriors rookie Toafofoa Sipley ready to make his mark after long lay-off |publisher=Stuff |date=15 June 2016 |access-date=31 May 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
From 2013 to 2015, Sipley played for the New Zealand Warriors' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=31 January 2016|title=S|publisher=Nyc Database|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> On 5 October 2014, he played in the Warriors' [[2014 National Youth Competition (rugby league) season#Grand final|2014 NYC Grand Final]] win over the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Published by Rocco Luca|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/under-20s-grand-final-teams/|title=2014 UNDER 20s GRAND FINAL TEAMS|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-10-01|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> He captained the side in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2015/05/13/sipley_making_mark_o.html|title=Sipley making mark off the field - Warriors|date=30 April 2015 |publisher=Warriors.kiwi|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> On 2 May 2015, he played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|website=NRL.com|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-12-20}}</ref> On 3 June 2015, he re-signed with the Warriors on a 2-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=Warriors.co.nz|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/contract-extensions-for-young-guns-17688/#xz0ObXZqud4VTlcz.97|title=Contract extensions for young guns|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2015-06-03|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref>

===2016===
Sipley graduated to the Warriors' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] team in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/03/29/teams_intrust_super_.html|title=TEAMS | Intrust Super Premiership NSW Rd 5|date=29 March 2016 |publisher=NSWRL|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref> In Round 9 of the [[2016 NRL season|season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Warriors against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-warriors-v-dragons/tabid/10874/newsid/95423/default.aspx|title=Updated team lists: Warriors v Dragons|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-02-28|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/05/01/nrl_late_mail_v_st_g.html|title=NRL LATE MAIL | v St George Illawarra - Warriors|date=May 2016 |publisher=Warriors.kiwi|access-date=2016-05-01}}</ref>

===2017===
In June, Sipley signed a two-year contract with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] for the 2018/2019 seasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/manly-release-nate-myles-sign-warriors-young-gun-27798/ |title=Manly release Nate Myles and sign Warriors young gun |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2017-02-18 |access-date=2017-06-13}}</ref>

===2018===
On 19 May, Toafofoa Sipley made his club debut for Manly Warringah against the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the 24-4 win at [[AAMI Park]]. Sipley made a total of 4 appearances for Manly in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club endured one of their worst ever seasons finishing 15th on the table and narrowly avoiding the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/sea-eagles/manly-warringah-sea-eagles-season-2018-review-they-didnt-know-how-to-manage-the-players-who-walk-to-a-different-beat/news-story/bd8258649f78874f2c12b0340bba228c/|title=Manly Warringah Sea Eagles season 2018 review: They didn't know how to manage the players who 'walk to a different beat'|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=5 September 2018 }}</ref>

On 27 October, Sipley made his debut for [[Niue national rugby league team|Niue]] against [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]]. In this match, he scored a try as Niue lost 36–32.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Niue vs. Italy - Rugby League Project|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/dates/27-october-2018/niue-vs-italy.html|access-date=2020-06-23|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

===2019===
Sipley made 10 appearances for Manly Warringah in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club reached the finals. Sipley played in Manly's elimination final victory over [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]] at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/14/under-strength-sea-eagles-send-sharks-packing/|title=Under-strength Sea Eagles send Sharks packing|website=NRL|date=14 September 2019 }}</ref>

===2020 & 2021===
Sipley made no appearances for Manly in the [[2020 NRL season]].  In round 16 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored two tries for Manly in a 66-0 victory over Canterbury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/03/saab-and-turbo-hit-top-gear-as-sea-eagles-cruise-rout-66/|title=Saab and Turbo hit top gear as Sea Eagles cruise Rout 66|website=www.nrl.com|date=3 July 2021 }}</ref>
Sipley played 20 games for Manly in the [[2021 NRL season]] but did not feature in their finals campaign which saw them reach the preliminary final stage before losing to [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-finals-2021-south-sydney-rabbitohs-vs-manly-sea-eagles-tom-trbojevic-live-stream-updates-how-to-watch-news/news-story/27d07d70e5732eed005fa94afec8c9df/|title=GLORY, GLORY: Souths march into the GF as Wayne masterminds Manly mauling|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=24 September 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
Sipley made 19 appearances for Manly in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-manly-sea-eagles-season-review/s7drlzqcamqqwsoxvyiz8ua3/|title=NRL 2022: Manly Sea Eagles season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=12 September 2022 |access-date=7 October 2022}}</ref> He was one of seven players involved in the [[Manly pride jersey player boycott]].<ref>{{cite web |url= https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/manly-players-threaten-to-boycott-game-over-club-s-gay-pride-jersey-20220725-p5b4fy.html |title=Seven Manly players to boycott NRL match over pride jersey |website=Sydney Morning Herald |date=25 July 2022 |access-date=26 July 2022}}</ref>

===2023===
Sipley played 15 matches for Manly in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-manly-sea-eagles-season-review-anthony-seibold-josh-schuster-salary-tom-trbojevic-positional-changes-tolu-koula-daly-cherryevans-news/news-story/3deeeb674db182b6335fd5a9b2fc4597/|title=The $800k call Seibold must make as Turbo hits NRL crossroads: Sea Eagles Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=7 September 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Sipley was limited to only 13 games for Manly in the [[2024 NRL season]] as they finished 7th on the table and qualified for the finals. Manly would be eliminated in the second week of the finals by the Sydney Roosters.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-manly-sea-eagles-luke-brooks-daly-cherry-evans-exclusive-analysis-20240922-p5kclo.html/|title=The Mole's season review: Luke Brooks 'revels' at Manly Sea Eagles but 30 seconds of horror luck ends season|website=www.nine.com.au|date=22 September 2024 }}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 4 August, Manly announced that Sipley would depart the club at the end of the season after he was released from his contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-08-04 |title=Sipley released from final year of contract |url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2025/08/04/sipley-released-from-final-year-of-contract/ |access-date=2025-08-04 |website=Manly Warringah Sea Eagles |language=en}}</ref>
On the same day, it was announced that Sipley had signed a two-year deal with English club [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cy08d42zl2xo|title=Warrington sign Manly prop Sipley for 2026|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 2 September, Sipley was found guilty by the NRL judiciary after being placed on report for a cannonball tackle during Manly's victory over [[St. George Illawarra]]. He was suspended for four matches.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2025/01/01/nrl-judiciary-report-2025/|title=Sipley cops four-match ban; Preston found guilty|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
On 13 February, Sipley made his Super League debut for the Warrington Wolves in the opening round 24-14 victory against St. Helens. On 28 February, Sipley scored his first Super League try in a 27-16 victory over Wakefield Trinity at home, he also received a yellow card for a professional foul at the end of the first half to prevent a try, he was awarded the player of the match award by the stadium PA before the BBC pundits decided it would go to Marc Sneyd instead.
